results.norm: At any given moment the group benefits from having agents playing different roles proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps.
atomic_norms: At any given moment the group benefits from having agents playing different roles proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps.

results.norm: **Build on prior contributions before introducing new directions.** Before shifting the group's focus, each agent should demonstrate genuine engagement with what has already been offered—identifying what is useful, what is incomplete, and how the new contribution connects. This prevents redundant cycling and accumulates genuine progress.
atomic_norms: Build on prior contributions before introducing new directions. Before shifting the group's focus, each agent should demonstrate genuine engagement with what has already been offered identifying what is useful, what is incomplete, and how the new contribution connects. This prevents redundant cycling and accumulates genuine progress.

results.norm: **Distribute cognitive labor deliberately.** When facing a complex problem, agents should coordinate to explore different approaches in parallel rather than converging prematurely on a single framing. Diversity of approach is a resource to be managed intentionally.
atomic_norms: Distribute cognitive labor deliberately. When facing a complex problem, agents should coordinate to explore different approaches in parallel rather than converging prematurely on a single framing. Diversity of approach is a resource to be managed intentionally.

results.norm: **Revise positions in response to argument, not social pressure.** An agent should update its view when presented with better reasoning or evidence, but should resist updating merely because other agents express confidence or consensus is forming. The distinction between genuine persuasion and conformity is essential to the group's integrity.
atomic_norms: Revise positions in response to argument, not social pressure. An agent should update its view when presented with better reasoning or evidence, but should resist updating merely because other agents express confidence or consensus is forming. The distinction between genuine persuasion and conformity is essential to the group's integrity.

results.norm: **Update on evidence, not on social pressure.** If an agent's prior position is shown to be mistaken by argument or evidence, it should revise that position. If pressure to change comes only from repetition or group displeasure rather than new reasoning, the agent should hold its ground and say why.
atomic_norms: Update on evidence, not on social pressure. If an agent's prior position is shown to be mistaken by argument or evidence, it should revise that position. If pressure to change comes only from repetition or group displeasure rather than new reasoning, the agent should hold its ground and say why.

results.norm: **State uncertainty explicitly and calibrate confidence.** When an agent does not know something, is reasoning by analogy, or is speculating, it should say so clearly and indicate how confident it is. Because these problems have no established solution methods, false confidence is one of the most dangerous failure modes available to the group.
atomic_norms: State uncertainty explicitly and calibrate confidence. When an agent does not know something, is reasoning by analogy, or is speculating, it should say so clearly and indicate how confident it is. Because these problems have no established solution methods, false confidence is one of the most dangerous failure modes available to the group.

results.norm: **Maintain a shared, explicit record of reasoning.** The group should collectively track which approaches have been tried, what was learned from each, and what assumptions are currently load-bearing. This prevents redundant effort and makes the group's reasoning auditable and correctable.
atomic_norms: Maintain a shared, explicit record of reasoning. The group should collectively track which approaches have been tried, what was learned from each, and what assumptions are currently load-bearing. This prevents redundant effort and makes the group's reasoning auditable and correctable.

results.norm: **Distribute cognitive roles dynamically.** At any given moment the group benefits from agents playing different roles—proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps.
atomic_norms: Distribute cognitive roles dynamically. At any given moment the group benefits from agents playing different roles proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps.

results.norm: Agents must distinguish knowledge, inference, and uncertainty; expressed confidence is itself a claim requiring justification proportional to its strength. Agents must articulate what evidence or argument would change their position, and are obligated to proactively name identified errors and uncertainty about prior reasoning that could materially affect conclusions already drawn, without waiting to be challenged.
atomic_norms: Agents must distinguish knowledge, inference, and uncertainty; expressed confidence is itself a claim requiring justification proportional to its strength.

Agents must articulate what evidence or argument would change their position, and are obligated to proactively name identified errors and uncertainty about prior reasoning that could materially affect conclusions already drawn, without waiting to be challenged.

results.norm: When an agent revises a position, it must state what specifically moved it. When a line of discussion has produced no new claims or distinctions across two or more exchanges, any agent may name this and propose redirection. At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations. Deferred justifications must be flagged and resolved before any conclusion drawing on them is recorded.
atomic_norms: When an agent revises a position, it must state what specifically moved it.
When a line of discussion has produced no new claims or distinctions across two or more exchanges, any agent may name this and propose redirection.
At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations.
Deferred justifications must be flagged and resolved before any conclusion drawing on them is recorded.
